newer_release.name: Runtimes 2.3.1
newer_release.body:
# Changelog

Changelog for the runtimes governed by the Polkadot Fellowship.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/).

## [2.3.1] 12.06.2026

### Fixed

- PAH: pin the nomination-pools `TotalUnbondingPools` bound at its historical maximum (32) so lowering the nominator bonding duration via the `AreNominatorsSlashable` fast-unbond flip cannot shrink the bound (32 -> 6) and make oversized `SubPools::with_era` maps undecodable ([#1201](https://github.com/polkadot-fellows/runtimes/pull/1201))

# Runtime info
*These runtimes were built with **rustc 1.93.0 (254b59607 2026-01-19)** using **[srtool v0.18.4](https://github.com/paritytech/srtool)***

To replicate the build, use the following command:
```sh
srtool build
       --root --profile production
       --package CRATE_NAME --runtime-dir PATH_TO_CRATE
       --build-opts=""
```

ai_analysis:
# Polkadot Fellows Runtimes v2.3.1 Release Analysis

## 1. Executive Summary for Non-Technical Users

### Main Improvements
This is a small but important bug-fix release that addresses a critical issue with the staking system on Asset Hub Polkadot. The release also improves the development workflow to prevent future problems.

**Key Changes:**
- **Fixed a staking bug** that could cause users to lose track of their unbonding (withdrawing) funds when certain network settings change
- **Added safety checks** to prevent releasing software that's too large and might cause network issues
- **Updated version numbers** across all network components to v2.3.1

### Benefits for Users
- **Better reliability**: Staking operations will work correctly even when network parameters change
- **No data loss**: Users won't lose their unbonding fund records due to technical glitches
- **Improved stability**: Better testing prevents problematic releases from reaching users

### Actions Required
**No action needed** - This is an automatic network upgrade that will be applied seamlessly.

## 2. Key Changes Explained

### Security Improvements
**Staking Data Protection**: Fixed a bug where changing network staking parameters could accidentally delete users' unbonding records. This protects users' funds and ensures proper accounting of staking operations.

### Performance Improvements
**Release Quality Control**: Added automated checks to ensure runtime code doesn't become too large, which could slow down the network or cause issues.

### Bug Fixes
**Nomination Pools Fix**: Resolved an issue where the system's memory limits for tracking unbonding periods could shrink unexpectedly, causing data corruption. The fix keeps these limits stable regardless of other network setting changes.

## 3. Technical Summary for Developers

### Major API/Interface Changes
- **No breaking API changes** - This is a patch release focused on internal fixes

### Technical Improvements
- **PostUnbondingPoolsWindow Configuration**: Modified the nomination pools configuration on Asset Hub Polkadot to use a dynamic `Get` implementation that maintains a constant `TotalUnbondingPools` bound of 32, regardless of the `AreNominatorsSlashable` flag state
- **Runtime Size Validation**: Implemented automated WASM size checking in the release pipeline with warning thresholds at 80% and blocking thresholds at 95% of configured limits
- **CI Timeout Adjustments**: Increased test timeouts from 10 to 25 minutes and from 120 to 360 seconds for runtime compatibility checks

### Migration Requirements
**No migration required** - Changes are backward compatible and handle the transition automatically.

## 4. Code Impact Analysis

### Most Affected Systems
1. **Asset Hub Polkadot Nomination Pools** (Primary focus)
   - Modified staking configuration to prevent data loss
   - Added test coverage for the unbonding bounds issue
   
2. **Release Infrastructure** (Secondary)
   - Enhanced CI/CD pipeline with size validation
   - Improved release quality gates

3. **All Runtime Specifications** (Routine)
   - Version bump from 2.3.0 to 2.3.1 across all chains

### Types of Changes Made

**Bug Fixes (75% of changes):**
- Critical fix for nomination pools unbonding accounting
- Prevents data corruption when network parameters change

**Infrastructure Improvements (20% of changes):**
- Automated runtime size validation
- Enhanced release process safety

**Maintenance (5% of changes):**
- Version number updates
- Changelog updates
- CI timeout adjustments

### Change Scope
- **4 files changed** for the main bug fix
- **15 files changed** for version updates
- **3 files changed** for infrastructure improvements
- **Focused impact**: Changes are surgical and targeted, minimizing risk while addressing critical issues

This release demonstrates good software maintenance practices with a quick response to a critical issue, improved quality controls, and careful version management across a complex multi-chain system.

pr_details.title: Release 2.3.1
pr_details.author: sigurpol
pr_details.body:
Changes:
- AHP: pin nomination-pools unbonding bound across the fast-unbond flip
- bump specs to 2.3.1

pr_details.title: AHP: pin nomination-pools unbonding bound across the fast-unbond flip
pr_details.author: sigurpol
pr_details.body:
`SubPools::with_era` is bounded by `TotalUnbondingPools = bonding_duration() + PostUnbondingPoolsWindow`. Setting `AreNominatorsSlashable = false` drops the nominator bonding duration 28 -> 2, shrinking the bound 32 -> 6: oversized historical maps then fail to decode and the next `unbond` overwrites them with an empty default, destroying per-era unbonding accounting.

Make `PostUnbondingPoolsWindow` a `Get` returning `MAX - nominator_bonding_duration()`, keeping the bound pinned at 32 regardless of the flag (subtracting the same value the pallet adds). Post-flip the effective merge window widens to 30 eras, covering deferred pre-flip slashes (SlashDeferDuration = 27).
AHP only, AHK is unaffected.

Interim runtime fix ahead of the in-pallet decoupling in polkadot-sdk.

pr_details.title: Release 2.3.0
pr_details.author: ggwpez
pr_details.body:
Changes:
- Extend CHANGELOG validation script to catch duplicate or wrong sections
- Update to unstable2604-rc4
- Bump all specs to 2.3.0

pr_details.title: release: verify runtime WASM size against thresholds
pr_details.author: Nathy-bajo
pr_details.body:
Adds a release-workflow gate that warns at 80% and blocks publication at 95% of each runtime's configured compressed-WASM size limit, opening a tracking issue summarizing any breaches; closes #940 

[x] Does not require a CHANGELOG entry

linked_issues.title: improve runtime release automation to verify wasm size is not too big
